The '''rockhopper''' was a bipedal riding animal.

==Overview==
Rockhoppers were believed to be a genetic cousin of the [[Cracian thumper]], which had evolved from transplanted flocks from their homeworld. They had a [[Horse/Legends|horse]]-like face and mane, short forelimbs, and a long, thick tail to maintain balance.<ref name="TRG" /> Rockhoppers lacked the claws of their Cracian ancestors, but had very strong jaws filled with sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]].<ref name="TUR">''[[The Unknown Regions]]''</ref>

They were most commonly found on [[Roon/Legends|Roon]], although specimens of them could also be found on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Biitu]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]].<ref name="TUR" />

==History==
[[File:Rockhopper-TGH1.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Mungo Baobab]]'s animal on Biitu.]]
Rockhoppers were used as domesticated riding animals for personal transport as well as sporting events. They were ridden in the [[Drainsweeper/Legends|Drainsweeper]] event of the [[Roon Colonial Games]] on Roon.<ref name="TRG" />

On Biitu, they were used as riding animals.<ref name="TGH" />

[[Adam Swiftgale]], a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], was a rockhopper jockey.<ref name="Rogues Gallery Star Wars Insider">{{InsiderCite|146|Rogues Gallery|Rogues Gallery: Clearance for Immediate Launch}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Their appearance was similar to that of Cracian thumpers, perhaps suggesting a common descent. This was confirmed in ''[[The Unknown Regions]]'', where it was stated that the Rockhopper was believed to be a genetic cousin of the Cracian thumper.
